packageRules.description: Loki and Mimir validation imports drive the operator's validation behavior. Both are tracked as main-branch digests (pseudo-versions) and bumped together so their shared github.com/grafana/dskit stays consistent. Mimir has no importable release tag (see the >=2.0.0 rule below) and Loki's release tags lag Mimir's main by months, so pairing a Loki *release* with a Mimir *main* digest fails to compile — dskit changed the dns.NewProvider signature and the two sides skew (PR #29). E2e suite must pass before merge.

github.com/grafana/mimir was forked from Cortex in 2022 and started its own versioning at v2.0.0. The pre-fork v1.x tags still exist in the repo but declare their module path as github.com/cortexproject/cortex, so Go module resolution refuses to import them as github.com/grafana/mimir. Constrain Renovate to true Mimir versions only — PR #8 (closed) demonstrated this when Renovate proposed v1.10.0.

Loki is intentionally tracked on a main-branch pseudo-version (base v3.0.0-<date>-<hash>), not its release tags. Loki cuts release tags (v3.6.x, v3.7.x, ...) from release branches that are NOT ancestors of main, so Go derives a v3.0.0-prerelease pseudo-version for main commits — which sorts BELOW those tags. Without this guard Renovate would 'upgrade' Loki from the main digest to the latest release tag (e.g. v3.7.2), whose dskit pin lags Mimir's main and breaks the build (dns.NewProvider skew — PR #29). '<3.0.0' blocks the release tags while still allowing same-track digest bumps (the analogous Mimir '>=2.0.0' rule likewise does not block its v1.3.1-0 digest bumps). Revisit if Loki cuts a release that catches up to Mimir's main dskit. NOTE: Loki migrated Limits parsing from gopkg.in/yaml.v2 to go.yaml.in/yaml/v4 after v3.7.2; that obsoleted the former gopkg.in/yaml.v2 pin rule (PR #10), which has been removed.

These are upstream-fork pins in go.mod `replace` directives that coordinate with the Loki and Mimir bumps. `go mod tidy` refreshes them as a side effect of bumping grafana/loki/v3 or grafana/mimir — Renovate should not propose them independently. PR #2 (closed) demonstrated this: Renovate proposed a digest where the fork had already moved to v4, breaking the v3 replace.
Actions are SHA-pinned with a `# vX.Y.Z` tag comment. Renovate updates both the SHA and the comment together (helpers:pinGitHubActionDigests preset + pinDigests:true).
